title: The Colonial Playbook: How Muslim Civilizations Were Weakened
description:
Anchor Series #9 | 01/20/26 – The Impact of Colonization on the Muslim World

#YasirQadhi #Colonialism #Civilization 

In the opening lecture of a new Anchor Module, Shaykh Yasir Qadhi examines how colonialism transformed the Muslim world politically, economically, intellectually, and religiously. Tracing the rise of European dominance, he outlines the five major phases of colonialism and explores how Muslim lands were weakened through military conquest, economic exploitation, the dismantling of Islamic institutions, and the creation of modern nation-states.

The lecture also examines the lasting effects of colonialism on Muslim thought and identity, explaining how the disruption of traditional systems contributed to the emergence of secular nationalism, socialism, modernism, progressivism, and fundamentalism. Shaykh Yasir concludes by highlighting Islam’s dynamic relationship with culture and setting the stage for further discussion in the series.

0:00:00 - Introduction
0:00:18 - Colonialism's Impact on the Ummah
0:02:12 - How Colonialism Shaped Modern Islam
0:04:00 - Overview of Colonialism's History and Phases
0:04:51 - Phase 1: European Maritime Ascendancy

0:07:04 - The Fall of Andalus (1492)
0:08:06 - European Exploration and Sea Power
0:10:17 - Economic Encroachment on Muslim Lands

0:30:27 - Italy in Ethiopia and Libya
0:32:23 - Phase 5: Neocolonialism
0:33:44 - Independence Without True Autonomy

0:36:37 - The Power of Global Corporations
0:38:00 - Resistance to Global Hegemony
0:38:22 - Control of Muslim Resources
0:39:06 - Colonialism's Lasting Impact
0:40:24 - Impact 1: Loss of Political Sovereignty
0:43:28 - Impact 2: Military and Cultural Inferiority
0:45:30 - Impact 3: Transformation of Islamic Institutions
0:46:49 - Few Muslim Lands Escaped Colonization

title: Animal Cruelty, Dunya vs. Akhirah & Other Trending Questions
description:
Q&A from Anchor Series #3 | 9/16/25  

In this Anchor Series session, Yasir Qadhi responds to a wide range of thoughtful questions that touch on theology, ethics, culture, and Muslim life in the modern world. From the problem of suffering to everyday social practices, this discussion grounds complex issues in Islamic principles while remaining practical and accessible.

0:00 Q1: Can you talk about the suffering of animals?
1:26 Q2: To what extent do we try to make sense of our trials?
3:21 Q3: Western Philosophers frequently discussed theodicy because they borrowed from the Greeks. Muslims also inherited from the Greeks, so why wasn't it as frequently discussed?
4:02 Q4: What should we do in societies where it is polite to bow to other people?
5:05 Q5: Is it permissible to kill animals for research purposes?
7:35 Q6: Birthday Parties?
8:29 Q7: Should the US be our home?

title: Overthinking Jannah? Watch This...
description:
Anchor Series #8 | 12/16/25 – Soteriology: The Study of Salvation Pt. 4

In the fourth installment of the Soteriology series, Shaykh Yasir Qadhi explores how attainable salvation truly is in Islam. Drawing from the Qur'an and authentic hadith, he explains the balance between Allah's mercy and justice, clarifies the minimum requirements for entering Jannah, and distinguishes them from the qualities that lead to its highest ranks. The lecture concludes with a powerful reminder that Allah has made the path to salvation accessible, and that sincere faith and striving are at its core.

0:00:00 - Introduction
0:01:06 - Recap of the Soteriology Series
0:02:49 - Why Perennialism Doesn't Make Sense

title: HOT Topic: All Non-Muslims Belong in Hell?
description:
Anchor Series #7 | 12/09/25 – Soteriology: The Study of Salvation Pt. 3

In the third installment of the Soteriology series, Shaykh Yasir Qadhi explores the fate of those outside Islam and the Sunni approach to salvation. Drawing from the Qur'an, classical scholarship, and major theological schools, he discusses those who never encountered Islam, received a distorted message, or rejected it for various reasons. The lecture highlights the importance of humility, nuance, and trust in Allah's perfect justice and mercy when speaking about the Hereafter.

00:00 - Introduction
00:26 - Why Soteriology Matters
01:35 - Worldly Judgments vs. Hereafter Verdicts
03:21 - Imam al-Tahawi on Individual Salvation
06:06 - Even al-Hajjaj Was Not Declared in Hell
07:36 - Abu Bakr al-Isma'ili's Position
09:06 - The Hardline View on Non-Muslims
09:50 - Islam Is the Only Accepted Religion

title: What No One Tells You About Aging Parents
description:
Special Session | 12/02/25 – The Reversal of Roles: Caring for Parents as They Age

In this heartfelt session, Shaykh Yasir Qadhi reflects on the reality of aging parents and the responsibilities that come with caring for them. Drawing from personal experience, he shares practical and spiritual advice on navigating parental decline, preserving their dignity, and balancing family responsibilities. He reminds us that true piety is not found in abstract debates, but in how we honor and serve our parents, and that as we treat them, so too will we one day be treated.

0:00:00 Introduction
0:01:23 The Reversal of Roles
0:03:05 We will eventually Decline
0:08:29 Every Kind of Death has its Benefits
0:10:48 "It's Always Somebody Else's Parents, isn't it?"
0:14:35 Becoming an Adult isn't as fun as it seemed
0:15:48 Point #1: Start Forming a new relationship with your parents
0:18:19 Once the elders are gone, a treasure of your heritage will be gone
0:19:37 Your Parents aren't used to talking to you as an adult either
0:20:31 You can form a unique bond even if they'll never be your friend
0:21:41 You will find a new level of respect for your parents
0:23:00 Point #2: Balance all of your responsibilities simultaneously
0:26:42 Point #3: You will need to attain Health Literacy
0:28:51 Health Literacy can be a manner of life and death
0:30:01 When your Parents begin the decline
0:31:35 When Shaykh's Father went through Dimentia
0:33:10 Point #4: The Family will need to discuss expectations for the future
0:35:19 Discuss the will and ways to protect your parents' wealth from the government
0:37:40 Discuss the DNR
0:41:02 One Sibling will do more than others
0:43:01 Point #5: Legal Issues like Power of Attorney
0:47:17 Power of Health Proxy
0:48:35 Point #6: Financial Realities
0:49:29 Healthcare in America sucks if you're not a multimillionaire
0:51:42 You can't donate more than one-third of your wealth
0:53:27 Point #7: Parenting your Parent will be the most awkward phase
0:57:11 Give them the Illusion of Freedom of much as possible to not strip their dignity
0:58:14 Love and Duty only take you so far, Faith in Allah takes you all the way
1:02:33 The Reality of Retirement Facilities
1:04:26 You may burnout but Allah gives you something
1:06:28 All of these theological debates are meaningless, real Piety is demonstrated by your treatment of parents
1:07:49 Shaykh Yasir's Father Sacrificed much for his parents
1:08:55 As you've treated your parents you too will be treated
1:09:47 Cherish your parents as much as you can
